questions.question: What are the two ways to get a Git repository?
What does the command 'git init' do?
options: Downloads a repository from a remote server
Creates a new Git repository from scratch
Pushes changes to a remote server
questions.answer: Creates a new Git repository from scratch

questions.question: What does the command 'git clone <url>' do?
options: Creates an empty repository at the given URL
Copies only the latest version of files from a remote repo
Copies an existing repository from a remote server, including its full history
Links your local repo to a remote without downloading anything
questions.answer: Copies an existing repository from a remote server, including its full history

questions.question: What is the purpose of a .gitignore file?
options: It lists files that should be deleted from the repository
It tells Git which files to never track
It prevents other users from cloning your repository
It hides files from the operating system's file explorer
questions.answer: It tells Git which files to never track

questions.question: In a .gitignore file, what do lines starting with '#' represent?
options: Patterns that apply only to directories

questions.question: In a .gitignore file, what does a trailing '/' on a pattern mean?
options: The pattern applies to the root directory only

questions.question: Which of the following is a common type of file to include in .gitignore?
Which .gitignore pattern would ignore all files with the .class extension?
Why should you add .env files to .gitignore?
options: They are too large for Git to handle
They contain environment-specific secrets that should never be committed
Git cannot track files that start with a dot
They cause merge conflicts every time
questions.answer: They contain environment-specific secrets that should never be committed

questions.question: Which .gitignore pattern would ignore the entire build/ directory?
After creating a .gitignore and adding files that match its patterns, what should 'git status' show for those ignored files?
options: They do not appear at all
They appear with a warning message
questions.answer: They do not appear at all

questions.question: Does .gitignore affect files that are already tracked by Git?
options: Yes, but only after the next commit
No, .gitignore only applies to untracked files
No, but Git will delete them automatically
questions.answer: No, .gitignore only applies to untracked files

questions.question: What is the default alias name for the remote server you cloned from?
Which command shows the remote URLs associated with a cloned repository?
What happens when you run 'git init' on a directory that is already a Git repository?
options: It deletes the existing repository and creates a new one
It prints an error and refuses to run
It reinitializes the existing repository without destroying anything
It creates a nested repository inside the existing one
questions.answer: It reinitializes the existing repository without destroying anything

questions.question: What message does Git display when you run 'git init' on an existing repository?
When you clone a repository, what do you receive?
options: Only the files from the latest commit
A complete copy of the repository with its full commit history
A read-only snapshot of the repository
Only the files, without any Git tracking
questions.answer: A complete copy of the repository with its full commit history

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a common category of files to put in .gitignore?
Which .gitignore pattern would ignore all files ending in .key?
What does the term 'distributed' mean in the context of Git cloning?
options: Changes are automatically sent to all team members
Each clone is a complete copy of the repository with full history
The repository is split across multiple servers
Only certain branches are available locally
questions.answer: Each clone is a complete copy of the repository with full history

questions.question: Which .gitignore entry would ignore the .idea/ directory used by JetBrains IDEs?
After setting up a .gitignore, you create a file src/App.java that does not match any ignore pattern. What does 'git status' show?
options: The file is ignored because .gitignore blocks all new files
The file appears as an untracked file
questions.answer: The file appears as an untracked file

questions.question: What is the correct order of steps to create a new repository with an initial commit?
options: git commit, git add, git init
git init, git add, git commit
git clone, git add, git commit
git add, git init, git commit
questions.answer: git init, git add, git commit

questions.question: Which of the following patterns in .gitignore would ignore the node_modules/ directory?
Should the .gitignore file itself be committed to the repository?
options: No, Git automatically reads it without committing
Yes, so that all contributors share the same ignore rules
No, each developer should maintain their own local copy
Only if the repository is public
questions.answer: Yes, so that all contributors share the same ignore rules
